fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer audiologist license to north carolina, audiologist compact north carolina, north carolina audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
board.parent_dept: NC Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series administered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in North Carolina.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the GovOS Professional Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the GovOS Professional Licensing Portal, complete the Audiologist license application, and pay the $60 application fee and $25 jurisprudence exam fee.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official graduate transcripts, official Praxis exam scores, and verification of your ASHA Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC-A) or American Board of Audiology (ABA) Certification to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete the North Carolina Jurisprudence Exam online, which is part of the application process within the GovOS Professional Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the Board's instructions to complete a criminal background check (CBC).

steps.title: Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Your complete application will be reviewed by Board staff and then presented to the Board at its next scheduled meeting for approval.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. North Carolina participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in North Carolina without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in North Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in North Carolina, new applicants must register and complete the online application through the GovOS Professional Licensing Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the GovOS Professional Licensing Portal, click 'Apply for a License' on the left sidebar, and then select 'New User?' to create an account with a valid email address and password.
